what_it_does: Defines a system prompt for layout, UI, and slide generation that consumes tokens.yaml and maps warmth, register, density, and modality into visual rules for surfaces, accents, structure, details, patterns, and failure conditions.
observations: Names four input tokens: warmth, register, density, and modality.
Specifies near-white surface colors that shift by warmth, with warmth treated as undertone rather than a visible color block.
Defines a gold-to-bronze-to-charcoal accent hierarchy with gold restricted to punctuation and one key emphasis.
Frames bilateral split layouts as the core structure, pairing showcase content with articulation content.
Includes exact detail rules for borders, radii, shadows, architectural texture, and diagonal hatching.
Lists failure conditions focused on avoiding large color fields, gold fills, cramped spacing, generic dashboards, and imprecise decoration.
standouts: Warmth at 60 or higher switches the hero background to a generated render rather than a CSS gradient.
Gold is constrained to sparse emphasis, never panel or background fill.
Negative space, bilateral layout, and one focal point are treated as structural rules.
conventions: Uses uppercase section headings and terse imperative design rules.
Combines precise numeric constraints with qualitative visual judgments.
Defines semantics through small marks, hairlines, dots, chips, outlines, and spatial relationships rather than colored panels.
Uses fail-if criteria as a guardrail section for generation quality.
concerns: Depends on tokens.yaml and prompt-image.txt without defining their schema or fallback behavior in this file.
Some failure terms are subjective, such as "generic SaaS dashboard" and "decorative not precise".
The warmth threshold for generated render use is explicit, but transition behavior below and above that threshold is not described.
notes: Brief someone that this file is a prescriptive visual-system prompt for warm, precise, sparse layout generation.
questions: What exact values and ranges can warmth, register, density, and modality take?
What does prompt-image.txt require for generated hero backgrounds?
How should generation behave when tokens.yaml omits one of the named tokens?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a layout/UI/slide generation system prompt with concrete visual rules and failure conditions.
intention_signals: It appears intended to keep generated slide and UI layouts warm, precise, spacious, and visually disciplined while preventing decorative color-block or dashboard-like outcomes.
novelty: The file treats warmth as an undertone variable, tightly limits gold to punctuation-level emphasis, and encodes failure modes as visual guardrails.
contribution: Contributes a neutral visual grammar for layout generation: near-white surface handling, restrained accent hierarchy, bilateral showcase/articulation composition, etched detail rules, semantic hairline patterns, and explicit visual failure checks.
summary_for_embedding: A system prompt for layout, UI, and slide generation that turns token values into restrained warm surfaces, sparse gold accents, bilateral composition, etched details, relationship diagrams, and visual failure rules.
intention_for_embedding: This was for guiding generated layouts toward precise, warm, spacious presentation design while constraining color use, emphasis, structure, and decorative effects.
